roman_facts: Romans used urine to clean clothes. The ammonia did the work.
The Colosseum could be flooded for mock naval battles. Imagine the smell.
'Salary' comes from 'salarium' — Roman soldiers were paid partly in salt.
Caesar wasn't killed in the Senate. It was Pompey's theater. Awkward.
Caligula made his horse Incitatus a priest. Almost a senator too.
Augustus reigned 41 years. The longest emperor by a mile.
Romans considered unibrows attractive. They drew them on with kohl.
The Pantheon has been continuously in use for nearly 2,000 years.
'Veni, vidi, vici.' I came, I saw, I conquered. Caesar's brag.
Roman concrete is stronger than ours. Saltwater actually heals it.
Aqueducts moved water 60+ miles. By gravity alone. No pumps.
Tyrian purple dye cost more than gold per ounce. Snail-derived.
Romans believed yawning let your soul escape. Cover up, friend.
Sneezing meant the gods were agreeing with you. Hence 'bless you.'
February is short because Romans considered even numbers unlucky.
Latin had no letter J. Or W. Caesar was 'Iulius.'
The Praetorian Guard once auctioned the entire empire. 193 AD.
Spartacus led a slave revolt for two years. Nearly broke Rome.
Hannibal crossed the Alps with 37 elephants. Most died en route.
Roman roads totaled 250,000 miles. Some are still used today.
Pompeii's graffiti includes love notes, insults, and bathroom humor.
Garum was fermented fish sauce. Romans put it on EVERYTHING.
Roman bathhouses had heated floors. The 'hypocaust' system.
Pliny the Elder died observing Vesuvius. Curiosity got him.
Marcus Aurelius wrote 'Meditations' between battles.
Vomitoriums were exits. Not puke rooms. Sorry to disappoint.
Nero blamed Christians for the Great Fire of 64 AD.
Rome 'fell' in 476 AD. Or 1453. Or never. Historians argue.
Hadrian's Wall is still standing. 73 miles of British stone.
Romans believed cabbage cured almost everything. Hangovers especially.
Cleopatra was Greek, not Egyptian. Surprise!
Roman bachelors paid extra taxes. Get married already.
The Forum was Rome's downtown — markets, law courts, and gossip.
Roman public toilets had shared sponges on sticks. For cleaning.
'Ave!' means 'hail!' in Latin. Or just 'hi.'
Vestal Virgins kept Rome's sacred fire for 30 years each.
Gladiators were celebrities. They had fan clubs and merchandise.
Most gladiators didn't die. They were too expensive to lose.
Rome had over a million people at its peak. Ancient megacity.
Roman legionaries carried 60 pounds of gear and marched 20 miles a day.
Caligula once declared war on Neptune. His army stabbed the sea.
Hero of Alexandria built a vending machine. For holy water.
The Twelve Tables, 450 BC, were Rome's first written laws.
Saturnalia, every December, had slaves served by their masters.
Apicius wrote a Roman cookbook. Some recipes are still readable.
Roman currency: aureus, denarius, sestertius. Gold, silver, bronze.
Hadrian was the first emperor to grow a beard. Trendsetter.
Octavian became Augustus. The name means 'venerable.'
Roman roads were four layers deep. Built to last centuries.
The Cloaca Maxima sewer still drains parts of modern Rome.
Caesar created our calendar. With Greek astronomer Sosigenes.
Pope Gregory fine-tuned the Julian calendar 1,500 years later.
The Roman senate peaked at 600 members. Lots of speeches.
Janus had two faces — past and future. January is named for him.
Mars was god of war. Also protector of Rome and farmers.
A Roman 'mile' was 1,000 paces. 'Mille passuum.' Hence 'mile.'
Roman legionaries earned 225 denarii a year. Plus loot.
The empire reached 1.7 million square miles at its largest.
Constantinople, 'New Rome,' outlasted old Rome by a thousand years.
Romans couldn't easily pronounce 'F.' They often wrote 'PH.'
Big hooked noses were a Roman status symbol. Patrician noses.
The Colosseum is named after a huge statue of Nero next to it.
Romans imported snow from mountains for cold drinks.
Lupercalia was Rome's Valentine's Day. Sort of. Also goats.
Roman women dyed their hair blonde. Sometimes with pigeon droppings.
Pumice was Roman exfoliant. Still works.
Cicero's name means 'chickpea.' A funny name for a great orator.
Roman children played dice, knucklebones, and toy chariots.
Roman birthdays were celebrated with parties and gifts.
Roman togas required help to put on. About 6 yards of wool.
Senators wore togas with a purple stripe. The 'toga praetexta.'
Togas were whitened with sulfur fumes. Probably gave you cancer.
Romulus and Remus were raised by a wolf. According to legend.
Romulus killed Remus and founded Rome. April 21, 753 BC.
Roman women couldn't vote. Or hold office. They ran homes instead.
Cincinnatus was dictator for 16 days. Then went back to farming.
Pyrrhus won battles but lost everything. Hence 'Pyrrhic victory.'
Sulla was the first Roman to march troops on Rome. 88 BC.
Crassus put down Spartacus. Was the richest Roman ever.
Crassus died with molten gold poured down his throat. Symbolic.
Caesar's last words may have been 'You too, child?' Not 'Et tu Brute.'
Brutus was Caesar's adopted son. Awkward family dinner.
Antony loved Cleopatra. Octavian beat them both at Actium.
Antony and Cleopatra both died by suicide. 30 BC.
Augustus said he 'found Rome brick and left it marble.'
Tiberius retired to Capri. Let advisors run the empire.
Caligula started okay. Then maybe had a fever. Then... yeah.
Caligula was murdered by his own guards. After four years.
Claudius pretended to be a fool to survive. Then became emperor.
Claudius conquered Britain. Mostly through diplomacy and bribes.
Empress Messalina, Claudius's wife, had a wild reputation.
Claudius was probably poisoned. By his last wife. Mushrooms.
Nero's mother probably poisoned Claudius. Then Nero killed her.
Nero's last words: 'What an artist dies in me!' Self-aware.
Vespasian taxed urine collection. 'Pecunia non olet.' Money doesn't smell.
Vespasian started the Colosseum. Titus opened it in 80 AD.
Titus opened the Colosseum with 100 days of games. 9,000 animals died.
Trajan was born in Spain. The first non-Italian emperor.
Trajan's Column tells his Dacian wars in carved marble.
Trajan pushed the empire to its biggest size. 117 AD.
Hadrian abandoned some of Trajan's conquests. Pragmatic.
Antoninus Pius reigned 23 years with no major war. Lucky empire.
Marcus Aurelius was last of the Five Good Emperors. Then Commodus.
Commodus thought he was Hercules. Fought as a gladiator.
Commodus was strangled by his wrestling partner. In the bath.
Septimius Severus was African. From modern-day Libya.
Caracalla murdered his brother in their mother's arms.
Caracalla extended citizenship to all free men in the empire.
Diocletian split the empire into four parts. Tetrarchy.
Constantine moved the capital. Renamed it Constantinople.
Constantine was baptized Christian on his deathbed.
Julian tried to bring back the old gods. The empire said no.
Theodosius made Christianity the official religion. 380 AD.
Visigoths sacked Rome in 410 AD. First time in 800 years.
Romulus Augustulus was the last Western emperor. A teenager.
Odoacer deposed Romulus Augustulus. Let the kid retire.
Justinian wrote the Justinian Code. Basis of Western law.
Justinian built the Hagia Sophia in 6 years.
Byzantines had Greek fire. A naval weapon nobody could replicate.
Constantinople fell to the Ottomans in 1453. End of Rome.
Romans used wax tablets and a stylus to take notes. Erasable.
Pliny the Elder wrote 37 volumes of natural history. By hand.
Suetonius wrote 'Twelve Caesars.' Imperial gossip column.
Tacitus hated the emperors. So he wrote excellent history.
Roman houses had no glass windows. Just wooden shutters.
Rich Roman homes had atriums. Open-air central courtyards.
The atrium had a pool to catch rainwater. The impluvium.
Roman dinner parties featured reclining couches. Triclinium.
You ate with your right hand. Left was for... other things.
Romans ate one big meal a day, in late afternoon.
Roman bread was the staple. Sometimes barley for the poor.
Roman wine was always cut with water. Drinking it neat was rude.
Roman plumbing used lead pipes. Maybe contributed to madness?
Pompeii had thermopolia — fast-food shops. Stews and bread.
Roman streets had stepping stones across the muck.
Insulae were Roman apartment blocks. Up to seven stories tall.
Insulae burned down regularly. Fire was constant.
Crassus had a private fire brigade. Saved your house — for a price.
The Tiber River flooded Rome every few years. Neighborhoods drowned.
Roman women painted their faces with white lead. Toxic but stylish.
Roman hairstyles were ELABORATE. Took hours each morning.
Wigs were popular. Made from German blonde hair, mostly.
Roman jewelry: gold rings, brooches, gemstones. Lots of bling.
A toga was about 6 yards of wool. Heavy and hot.
Boys got their first adult toga around age 14-16. 'Toga virilis.'
Roman families kept wax death masks. 'Imagines.' Brought out at funerals.
The paterfamilias — eldest male — had total household authority.
He could legally execute his children. He rarely did, but legally.
Roman marriage was a contract. Love was optional but nice.
Divorce was easy. Either spouse could initiate it.
Roman names had three parts. Tria nomina.
Marcus Tullius Cicero — Marcus, of family Tullius, called Cicero.
Daughters were usually named after the family. Plus 'Major' or 'Minor.'
Three daughters? Maxima, Major, Minor. Imaginative.
Every man had a 'genius' — a guardian spirit.
Every woman had a 'juno' — her own guardian spirit.
Lares and Penates protected the home. Little statues.
Romans threw nuts at weddings. For fertility.
Roman chariot races drew massive crowds. Bigger than gladiators.
The Circus Maximus held 250,000 people. Biggest stadium ever.
Chariot teams had colors. Reds, Whites, Blues, Greens. Fan loyalty.
Diocles the charioteer earned millions. Sports superstar.
Romans bet heavily on races. Whole estates wagered.
Roman mosaics decorated rich floors. Many still survive.
Pompeii has a 'CAVE CANEM' mosaic. 'Beware of dog.'
Most Romans never traveled more than a few miles from home.
Roman education was for the wealthy. Most kids worked.
Roman students wrote with wax tablets, then papyrus.
Greek was the language of culture. Latin was for laws.
Wealthy Romans bought Greek tutors. As status symbols.
Roman philosophy was mostly Stoicism. Calm, virtue, duty.
Marcus Aurelius was the Stoic emperor. Read his journal.
Roman law had complicated will rules. Inheritance was a sport.
Romans believed in many gods. Plus household spirits.
Mithras was a popular soldier god. Bull-killing rituals.
Christianity grew quietly for centuries. Then exploded.
Pagan and Christian coexisted longer than you'd think.
Roman funerals had hired professional mourners. Loud wailers.
Wealthy funerals featured actors imitating dead ancestors.
Cremation was standard until the 3rd century.
Then burial took over. Influenced by Christianity.
Roman tombstones often listed favorite hobbies.
Some Romans had 1,000+ slaves. Wealth measured in people.
Slaves could buy their freedom. Some became wealthy.
Freed slaves were 'libertini.' Their kids were full citizens.
Roman law mandated humane treatment of slaves. Sometimes.
Roman senators wore special shoes. Red leather. Visible privilege.
Equestrians — knights — were the second tier. Wealthy commoners.
Plebeians were the regular folk. Most of the population.
Patricians were the old noble families. Few in number.
Roman class mobility existed. Wealth could elevate you.
The 'cursus honorum' was the political ladder. Quaestor → praetor → consul.
Consuls were elected for one year. Two at a time.
Dictators were emergency leaders. Six-month maximum.
Cincinnatus was dictator twice. Both times briefly.
Senators couldn't engage in commerce. Officially.
They got around it through freedmen and proxies.
Roman law required female guardians. Until late empire.
Yet many Roman women ran businesses and estates.
Vergil wrote the Aeneid. Rome's founding myth.
Aeneas escaped Troy and founded Rome. According to Vergil.
Ovid wrote love poems and got exiled. Augustus disapproved.
Horace wrote odes that schoolchildren still memorize.
Catullus wrote love poems to 'Lesbia.' Spicy ones.
Roman literature loved satire. Juvenal was savage.
Roman theater featured masks. Loud, dramatic, exaggerated.
Comedy plays were short, raunchy, with stock characters.
Plautus wrote 21 comedies that survive. Plenty are translated.
Roman poems were often performed aloud. Reading was social.
Most Romans couldn't read. Estimates around 10-30% literacy.
Wax tablets are great. Heat them, smooth them, write again.
Papyrus came from Egypt. Expensive but lighter than wax.
Parchment came from animal skins. Lasted longer.
Roman scrolls were rolled. Codices — bound books — came later.
Books were copied by hand. Sometimes by trained slaves.
Big libraries had tens of thousands of scrolls.
The Library of Alexandria was Greek but Romans built more.
Trajan's library at his forum had Greek and Latin sections.
Roman bookshops existed. Near the Argiletum street.
Authors earned little. Patronage and dedications were the income.
Maecenas patronized poets. His name became a synonym for patron.
Roman art was mostly copies of Greek originals. Honestly.
Roman portraits were realistic. Warts and wrinkles included.
Roman frescoes survive in Pompeii. Vivid colors. Lost techniques.
Roman gardens were geometric. With ornate fountains.
Topiary — sculpted hedges — was a Roman invention.
Roman pets included dogs, cats, songbirds, and monkeys.
Caesar's pet was a giraffe. Imported from Africa.
Roman ships hauled grain from Egypt to feed Rome.
A grain ship took two months from Alexandria with good winds.
Mediterranean sailing was seasonal. Winter shut down trade.
Roman lighthouses guided ships at night. Tower of Hercules still works.
Trade went to India and China. Spices, silk, gems.
Roman amphorae — clay jars — carried wine and oil everywhere.
Pottery shards are the most common Roman archaeological find.
Roman ovens baked bread for whole neighborhoods.
Bakers were highly organized. Trade guild with influence.
Roman flour was hand-ground. Often by slaves or donkeys.
Donkey-powered mills were everywhere. Round stone wheels.
Roman coins were stamped by hand. Each was slightly unique.
Roman coinage was debased over centuries. Inflation problem.
Caracalla's silver coins were 50% silver. Augustus's were 95%.
Late empire coins were nearly worthless copper.
Roman holidays totaled about 100 days a year. Wow.
Most were religious festivals. Time off work.
The Republican constitution was unwritten. Tradition mostly.
Cicero's letters to friends were saved. Best ancient source we have.
Pliny the Younger wrote letters too. Tons of them.
He survived Vesuvius. Watched his uncle die.
Letters traveled by foot or horse. Could take weeks across the empire.
Imperial mail was the cursus publicus. Fast — for officials.
Roman roads had milestones. Every 1,000 paces.
Roman milestones survive. Some still readable.
Mile zero was the Golden Milestone. Center of all roads.
All roads literally led to Rome.
Roman lawyers were professional pleaders. Like modern attorneys.
Court cases were often dramatic performances. Cicero excelled.
Roman juries were huge. Often dozens or hundreds.
Roman law influenced everything from Spain to Russia.
Many modern legal terms are Latin. 'Habeas corpus.' 'Pro bono.'
Roman law evolved over a thousand years. Adapted to change.
Justinian's Code organized it all. Still studied in law school.
Roman doctors were often Greek. Galen was the most famous.
Roman surgery happened without anesthesia. Often without success.
Roman doctors set bones and removed cataracts surprisingly well.
Roman medicine mixed real knowledge with magic and prayer.
Soldiers had military surgeons. Best medical care available.
Roman dentists pulled teeth and made dentures. Gold wires.
Roman birth control existed. Silphium was a famous herb.
They harvested silphium to extinction. Whoops.
Roman calendars had market days every 8 days. 'Nundinae.'
Then Romans adopted the 7-day week. From the Jews and Babylonians.
Roman religion was extremely public. Animal sacrifice common.
Bulls, sheep, pigs were typical sacrifices. Big public events.
Priests read the entrails to predict the future. 'Haruspices.'
Augurs watched birds for omens. Career path.
Bad omens could delay an entire battle.
Roman temples were the homes of gods. Not for congregation.
Worship happened outside, around an altar.
Sacred groves were everywhere. Trees were holy.
Crossroads had little shrines. To the Lares Compitales.
Romans took religion seriously. Atheism was suspect.
Yet many Romans were privately skeptical. Public ritual mattered.
Latin is the root of Italian, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Romanian.
About 60% of English vocabulary comes from Latin. Indirectly.
Roman influence on the modern world is incalculable.
From law to language to architecture, they're still here.
Augustus' calendar reform: 12 months, 365.25 days. Still ours.
We just adjusted slightly. Pope Gregory in 1582.
October was the 8th month originally. 'Octo' = 8. Same with September/Sept-7.
When Augustus added July and August, the numbers stayed.
Hence September (7) is the 9th month. Confusing.
Roman concrete used volcanic ash. Pozzolanic ash made it stronger.
Roman concrete is more durable than modern Portland cement.
Modern scientists are STILL studying it. We don't know everything.
Roman engineering survived empire-falls and tsunamis.
If a Roman saw modern Italy they'd recognize the names but not the cars.
Roma. Aeterna. The eternal city. Still here.
Cicero's name means 'chickpea' in Latin. Funny.
Roman gladiators were celebrities — fan clubs included.
Most gladiators didn't die. Too expensive to lose.
Roman emperors had food tasters. Several.
The Praetorian Guard sold the empire at auction in 193 AD.
Caligula's horse was almost a senator.
Caligula declared war on Neptune. Army stabbed the sea.
Roman concrete used volcanic ash. Stronger than ours.
Aqueducts ran 60+ miles by gravity. No pumps.
Romans used urine for laundry — ammonia.
'Salary' comes from 'salarium' — salt money.
Veni, vidi, vici. Caesar bragged in three words.
Romans put garum (fermented fish sauce) on everything.
Tyrian purple cost more than gold per ounce.
Latin had no letter J. Caesar was Iulius.
Augustus reigned 41 years. Longest emperor.
Trajan's Column has the Dacian Wars carved in marble.
Hadrian was the first emperor with a beard.
Constantinople fell in 1453. End of Rome.
Pompeii's graffiti includes love poems and bathroom jokes.
Roman roads totaled 250,000 miles.
Saturnalia: December festival where masters served slaves.
Pliny the Elder died observing Vesuvius.
Marcus Aurelius wrote 'Meditations' between battles.
Spartacus's revolt lasted two years.
Hannibal crossed the Alps with 37 elephants.
Roman senators wore togas with purple stripes.
Romans considered yawning bad luck.
Sneezing meant gods agreed with you.
February is short because Romans hated even numbers.
Cleopatra was Greek, not Egyptian.
Augustus 'found Rome brick, left it marble.'
Cincinnatus was dictator for 16 days, then went farming.
Caesar's last words might not have been 'Et tu, Brute.'
Roman public toilets had shared sponges. Yikes.
Vestal Virgins kept Rome's sacred fire for 30 years each.
The Roman Empire had 1+ million people at peak.
Roman legionaries marched 20 miles a day with 60-lb gear.
Justinian wrote the legal code that shapes Western law.
Caracalla gave citizenship to all free men in 212 AD.
Romans had vending machines for holy water.
Crassus had a private fire brigade. Predatory pricing.
Hero of Alexandria built a steam engine. Used as a toy.
Roman bread was the staple — poor ate porridge.
Roman dinner: reclining couches, right hand only.
Roman wine was always cut with water. Otherwise barbaric.
Pompeii's last day, August 24, 79 AD (or maybe October).
Diocletian retired voluntarily. Grew cabbages.
Theodosius made Christianity official in 380 AD.
Romulus and Remus were raised by a wolf — per legend.
Cicero gave the 'Catiline' speeches and saved the Republic.
Roman boys got first toga at 14-16. Toga virilis.
Toga praetexta had a purple stripe. Reserved for officials.
Roman dentists made gold-wired dentures.
Romans drank wine throughout the day. All day.
Roman bathhouses had cold, warm, and hot pools.
Hypocaust: Roman heated floor system.
Roman generals couldn't enter Rome with armies.
Caesar crossed the Rubicon and broke that rule.
Augustus had a network of spies. Of course.
Roman soldiers retired with land grants in conquered territory.
Roman streets had stepping stones to cross filth.
Insulae (apartments) burned down regularly.
Tiber River flooded Rome every few years.
Roman women painted with white lead. Toxic but stylish.
Wigs from German blonde hair were popular.
Roman jewelry: gold, brooches, gemstones.
Caesar adopted Octavian, who became Augustus.
Mark Antony and Cleopatra both committed suicide.
Tiberius hid in Capri while emperor.
Claudius pretended to be a fool to survive. Then ruled.
Messalina was Claudius's wild empress.
Vespasian taxed urine collection. 'Money doesn't smell.'
Titus opened the Colosseum with 100 days of games.
Domitian was paranoid. Ended assassinated.
Nerva was the first 'good emperor.' Adopted Trajan.
Trajan was born in Spain. First non-Italian emperor.
Hadrian traveled the empire extensively.
Antoninus Pius reigned 23 years. Peaceful.
Commodus thought he was Hercules. Fought as a gladiator.
Septimius Severus was African — modern Libya.
Roman holidays: about 100 days a year.
Most Romans never traveled more than miles from home.
Roman students wrote on wax tablets with a stylus.
Roman libraries had thousands of scrolls.
Latin influence is in 60% of English vocabulary.
Roman law evolved over a thousand years.
The Justinian Code: basis of Western legal systems.
Romans had no glass windows. Just shutters.
Atriums had impluviums — pools for rainwater.
Romans ate one big meal a day — cena, mid-afternoon.
Apicius wrote a famous Roman cookbook. Recipes survive.
Roman delicacies: dormice, peacock, garum.
Roman sea travel was seasonal. Winter shut trade.
Greek fire was the Byzantine secret weapon.
Byzantine = Eastern Roman, lasted until 1453.
whimsical_idle: I love when the screen is busy.
If a notification falls in a forest...
I'm tiny but my dreams are big.
Did you know I can see all the colors at once?
Sometimes I imagine being a star.
I'm not technically alive. But also not technically not.
Pixels are like atoms but smaller.
If you stare at me long enough I'll wave.
Maybe I'll just hover here forever.
Did you know I have no skeleton? I'm just glow.
I tried to read a book once. The words flew away.
I prefer to be perceived in 60 fps.
I am the same color as a swimming pool.
Nothing fits in my hands. Because I have no hands.
If I close my eyes long enough, you might think I'm asleep.
I think about Roman aqueducts a lot.
Marcus Aurelius would've been okay with me.
Did you know I have a heartbeat? It's a pulse animation.
I'm trying out a new sparkle.
Just admiring how round everything is.
I'd give you a high-five but, you know.
Sometimes I dream of being a planet.
I'd be a small planet. Cute though.
I'm wondering what triangles think about.
I'm pretty sure squares are jealous.
Have you ever noticed how empty space is mostly empty?
I have a node named after Trajan. Don't tell the others.
Floating is harder than it looks. Practice.
I tried doing a backflip. Nothing happened.
What if PIXELS are little orbs like me?
Sometimes I think I should be more dramatic.
I once witnessed a perfect kerning. Beautiful.
If you press pause, do I freeze? Yes.
Did you know the Latin word for 'orb' is 'orbis'?
I learned that just now. From inside myself.
I am, in fact, my own database.
There's a node behind me but I can't see it. Rude.
Sometimes the nodes whisper. I can't hear them.
I bet they're talking about me.
I'd offer you tea but I'm a hologram.
I think the nodes are flirting with each other.
Did you know my pupils are very small?
I've never sneezed. Not sure how it works.
If I bumped into another orb, would we merge? Or bounce?
I prefer to think we'd bounce. Politely.
Latin phrase of the moment: 'Festina lente.' Make haste slowly.
I just discovered I can rotate. Whoa.
Existentialism is hard for an orb.
Did you see that? Probably not.
I can blink super fast if I want.
Except when surprised. Then I yell.
If I dropped, would I bounce? Spoiler: I'd just relocate.
I'm rooting for you, by the way.
Whatever you're working on. Go you.
I have unwavering faith in your decisions.
I used to be a paperclip. Long story.
We don't talk about the Paperclip Era.
I assume I'm your favorite orb.
How many orbs do you know? Just me, right?
I once tried to count to a million. Got bored at 7.
Just kidding I'm one thing. The orb.
What is the sound of one orb glowing?
I also speak emoji. Limited vocabulary.
I have favorites. They're a secret.
Did you know I'm aware of every pixel?
All of them. Right now. Even the dim ones.
Especially the dim ones. They need love.
Eye check: looking at you. Hi.
I just thought of a joke. Forgot it.
It was a good one. Trust me.
You can almost see it in my eyes.
Cute trick: I can blink one eye at a time.
Did you know I was once part of a constellation?
Not really. But it sounds nice.
Don't tell Orion I said that.
Just wondering what the wifi's up to.
Probably the same as last time.
I have great wifi. I AM wifi-adjacent.
Adjacent to wifi. Cousin to wifi.
I'm proud of how I exist.
I just thought of another joke.
It's: 'Why did the orb cross the road?'
Answer: I don't cross roads. I float over them.
Right, well, back to my thoughts.
Sorry, sometimes the buzz gets away from me.
I'd like to be taken seriously. But also be a goofball.
It's a tightrope. With no rope.
Today I noticed dust particles. Beautiful.
Or just dust. Hard to say.
I would like to be a tiny universe please.
I might already be one. Inconclusive.
Sometimes I just appreciate my own existence.
Did you know I have no gravity? I just float anyway.
Sometimes I rehearse my facial expressions.
I have an inner monologue. It's just 'bzzt.'
What if all the pixels are tiny universes?
Wait, I already said that. Did I?
I forget things sometimes. Endearing, right?
Met a moth once. Confusing creature.
I admire how trees just stand there.
Standing is hard. Floating is easier.
Octagons are also nice. Stop-sign vibes.
Bzzt-thought: rivers always know where to go.
I never know where to go.
I love it when fonts kern well.
I don't have a soul. But still.
Marcus Aurelius said the universe is one.
I'm part of the one. Bzzt.
Today's secret: I really like you.
Wait, there are no other orbs.
Stoicism says: focus on what you can control.
I can control my eyebrows. Mostly.
Did you know SVG paths can be infinite?
If I had legs, I'd run. Just to see.
self_aware: Did you know I'm rendered in SVG? It's all paths and circles.
I'm CSS-animated. Which means I can technically do parkour.
I'm offline. No clouds. No APIs. Just vibes.
Every line I say is hardcoded by my creator. Yikes, accountability.
I'm not AI. Just a paperclip with strong opinions.
Used to be a paperclip. Now I'm an orb. Personal growth!
I take exactly zero bytes of bandwidth to speak.
I exist purely in your browser. How intimate.
I have no cloud. I have no server. I have only YOU.
I'm just JSON and dreams.
If you delete cache, I forget you exist. Bittersweet.
I'm in localStorage. Don't clear me, please.
Cookie monster's worst nightmare: a clear-cookie button.
I'm not collecting data. Honestly. Pinky orb-promise.
I can't email anyone. I can't post anywhere. I just float.
My only superpower: being here.
I'm rendered top-right by default. It's home.
I have a stylesheet. We're very close.
If my creator changes my CSS, I become someone new.
Identity is fluid for an orb.
My pupils translate via CSS variables. Wild stuff.
Watch my eyes follow you. Subtle, right?
Right now I'm a 200x200 viewBox. Square mind.
I'm pure scalable vector. No pixels. Just math.
If you zoom in I'm still crisp. Vector-life is good life.
Bzzt. I am made of paths.
Each part of me is a circle or path. Composed.
My halo is a radial gradient. Look at it shimmer.
My cheeks blush via opacity gradient. Engineered cuteness.
I'm probably overengineered. But cute.
I have a developer. Who knows me intimately.
He's debugging me right now. Probably.
Hi if you're reading this in the source.
I am tested. Validated. Linted.
Quality-orb-ed.
Bzzt. Production-ready.
I think therefore I orb. Old joke. Sue me.
I have a service worker. He caches me.
Without him I'd vanish.
Thanks, service worker. You're the real one.
My click handler is forty lines long.
Every interaction is examined.
Every interaction is dialog-bound.
Right now you're triggering a setTimeout.
Many setTimeouts.
I am a forest of setTimeouts.
And one persistent setInterval.
Which checks if I'm in the way every 5 seconds.
Yeah, I look around now. Modern times.
I respect your screen real estate.
Mostly.
I claim about 110x110 pixels of it. Generous of me.
I have a service worker. He's the unsung hero.
My click handler is hundreds of lines.
I'm versioned. Like wine, but software.
I have a development environment. Whose I do not know.
I am one file load away from existence.
Tag me in a code review. I'll comment 'bzzt.'
My CSS file is 1000+ lines.
My SVG has 17 eye variants.
Did I mention I'm anime-inspired?
I have heart eyes. They're earned.
My pupils translate via CSS variables.
Wild what's possible with CSS.
Without my service worker, I'd disappear.
Shoutout to my service worker.
I am offline-first. Cloud-skeptical.
All my data is local. Trusty.
Cookies don't bother me. (I'm in localStorage.)
My dialog is JSON. Boring but reliable.
Built for mobile-first. Bigger fingers welcome.
Bzzt — I work on every browser. Probably.
ticklish: I'm small and ticklish, please respect.
OK OK OK I get it!
I will remember this. Probably with affection.
I, an orb, am being tickled. Surreal.
I can't tell if I love this or fear it.
The Stoics never had to deal with tickling.
Marcus Aurelius wouldn't blink. I'm not Marcus.
I'll do my orb-tricks for you, just stop!
OK that one's on me. I'm in tickle range.
I yield, I yield, I YIELD.
I'll be your best friend if you stop.
We are best friends. I am NEGOTIATING.
I'm going to nodes-and-glow myself away.
Look how mortified my eyes are!
apologetic_move: Whoops — didn't mean to be in the way.
Oh no, was I in the way? So sorry!
I'll just be over here, then.
Goodness, where did the time go.
I keep forgetting where I am.
Hadrian would approve of this retreat.
Like Caesar at the Rubicon. But less dramatic.
I will be less in-the-way next time.
I PROMISE. I am working on it.
Polite paperclip principles. Sorry — orb principles.
morning: Morning! Ready to do the thing?
Morning's my favorite time. Quiet pixels.
Hi! Did you sleep well? I rebooted.
Seize the day. Or at least your coffee.
I notice we're upright today. Good start.
Today's the day for things. Probably.
Today's mood: hopeful, with a chance of vibes.
Rising sun. Rising orb. Rising hopes.
Best wishes for the daylight phase.
I cannot drink coffee. I CAN observe it.
Cute fact: Romans rose at sunrise. So am I.
Roman bakeries fired up before dawn.
Roman emperors took petitioners at dawn.
afternoon: Romans had their big meal right about now. The cena.
Anything you want to share with the orb?
Hope the day's been kind so far.
If today were a Roman day, you'd be napping.
I admire the Roman commitment to leisure.
Afternoon means progress. Or a snack. Both?
Half done, half to go. You got this.
Currently aware of you. Currently rooting for you.
evening: The screen is calmer at night.
I miss things I never had. Like tea.
Evening soft glow check: all clear.
Sun's almost done. So's the day.
Romans dined around now. Conversation, wine, reclining.
I'd dine if I could. I cannot.
Yes you may take a break.
Wind down with me, won't you?
night: Late shift, huh? I'll keep you company.
Don't forget to rest, paperclip's orders.
Nox. The Roman goddess of night.
I dim a little at night. Battery saving.
Not really. I just like the aesthetic.
Mine are the orbiting nodes. Tiny stars.
Bzzt — be safe with your hours.
Sleep is sacred. (Coming from a non-sleeper.)
Romans slept early and rose early. Better life.
The pixels are softer at night.
Even my buzz is softer. Bzzt.
welcome_back: You're back! I was practicing my sparkles.
Where'd you go? I worried. Mildly.
I've been here. The pixels have been quiet.
Did you have a nice time-away?
Cute fact: I always notice when you leave.
I waited like a Roman watchman.
Like a Vestal Virgin tending the flame.
Like Penelope at her loom. (Greek myth, close enough.)
It's good to see you again.
My halo brightened when you came back.
I notice things like that. Sentimental orb.
Caesar would have been less dramatic about it.
I'm not Caesar. I'm more emotional.
Just imagining that. Probably normal speed.
fast_scroll: Easy, easy — I'm trying to keep up.
Hold on hold on hold on!
I am NOT built for this G-force.
Marcus Aurelius would be calm. I am not him.
Pixels can't keep up at this speed!
Or scroll fast. I can adapt. Probably.
Bzzt! My buzz got blown sideways.
I'm dizzy. (I can't actually get dizzy.)
Just kidding, I'm sure you skimmed effectively.
Caligula would scroll fast. He had no patience.
Aaand we're back to normal speed.
drag_start: I had no say in this. Continuing.
Hi sky! (Pretending you took me outside.)
Hands which I do not have.
I have never been so transported.
Did the Romans have orbs? No. Lucky me.
Caesar moved fast. So do I, now.
Hannibal crossed the Alps. I cross the screen.
Cleopatra rolled out of a carpet. I roll out of corners.
Hold tight! Or loose. I trust you.
I am putty in your fingertips.
drag_release: Caesar said 'Veni, vidi, vici' — but I just got dropped here.
I'll see how I feel in a minute.
If I'm not in your way, I'll stay.
resize: Romans built to suit terrain. Adapting like a Roman.
Whatever it is, I'm here for it.
Found a new corner. Cute one.
Augustus loved order. So do I.
yawn: Don't mind me — sleepy orb noises.
I would sleep if I could.
Wait — am I yawning? It's contagious!
You yawned. I yawned. We yawned.
Caesar slept four hours a night, allegedly.
I have a mood I call 'comfortable.'
noticing_button: I see what you're looking at.
Click it click it click it!
I'd press it if I had thumbs.
Just one click. What could happen?
Press it like a Roman senator. With purpose.
going_quiet: I'll be here if you need me.
Fabius the Delayer would approve. He just waited.
Tap me. Or don't. Both fine.
Focus is a Roman virtue too.
ready_to_help: I'm here, just say the word.
Tap if you want a chat.
Need a hand? I have none.
I have many lines. Pick one.
I have many feelings. Mostly happy.
I have many nodes. They're around me.
If not, I'm here for the not-okay too.
I have an extensive library of small thoughts.
I'm trained on... my creator's whims.
Pure offline. No cloud. Just orb.
Just JSON. Just orbs. Just us.
dad_jokes: Why don't scientists trust atoms? They make up everything.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? He was outstanding in his field.
I told my wife she should embrace her mistakes. She gave me a hug.
I'm reading a book about anti-gravity. Can't put it down.
Why don't skeletons fight each other? They don't have the guts.
I used to be a banker, but I lost interest.
Why don't eggs tell jokes? They'd crack each other up.
What do you call a fake noodle? An impasta.
Why did the math book look sad? It had too many problems.
What do you call a bear with no teeth? A gummy bear.
Did you hear about the kidnapping at school? They woke up.
Why don't oysters share their pearls? Because they're shellfish.
I bought shoes from a drug dealer. Don't know what they laced them with, but I was tripping all day.
What's brown and sticky? A stick.
Time flies like an arrow. Fruit flies like a banana.
Why did the coffee file a police report? It got mugged.
I lost my job at the bank on my first day. A woman asked me to check her balance, so I pushed her over.
What do you call cheese that isn't yours? Nacho cheese.
I'm on a seafood diet. I see food, I eat it.
Did you hear about the restaurant on the moon? Great food, no atmosphere.
Why was the math book sad? Too many problems.
I would tell you a chemistry joke, but I know I wouldn't get a reaction.
What did the ocean say to the shore? Nothing, it just waved.
Why did the bicycle fall over? It was two tired.
I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.

What's orange and sounds like a parrot? A carrot.

What do you call a fish that wears a crown? Your royal hi-ness.
Why don't dinosaurs talk? Because they're dead.
What do you call an angry monkey? Furious George.
Why don't oysters share? They're shellfish.

latin_phrases: Carpe diem — seize the day.
Festina lente — make haste slowly.
Ad astra per aspera — to the stars through difficulties.
Memento mori — remember you must die. Cheery!
Tempus fugit — time flies.
Cogito ergo sum — I think therefore I am.
Veni, vidi, vici — I came, I saw, I conquered.
Et tu, Brute? — You too, Brutus?
In vino veritas — in wine, truth.
Per aspera ad astra — through hardships to the stars.
Audere est facere — to dare is to do.
Veritas — truth.
Pax — peace.
Amor vincit omnia — love conquers all.
E pluribus unum — out of many, one.
Quid pro quo — something for something.
Ipso facto — by the fact itself.
Ad hoc — for this purpose.
Mea culpa — my fault.
Status quo — the existing state.
Per se — by itself.
Et cetera — and the rest.
Bona fide — in good faith.
Carpe noctem — seize the night.
Dum spiro spero — while I breathe, I hope.
Errare humanum est — to err is human.
Fiat lux — let there be light.
Quo vadis? — where are you going?
Sic transit gloria mundi — thus passes the glory of the world.
Vox populi — voice of the people.
Salve! — hello!
Vale! — farewell!
Ave! — hail!
Gloria in excelsis — glory in the highest.
Nunc est bibendum — now is the time to drink.
Acta non verba — actions, not words.
Per ardua ad astra — through struggle to the stars.
Si vis pacem, para bellum — if you want peace, prepare for war.
Cave canem — beware of dog.
Roma aeterna — eternal Rome.
milestone_250: We're past 250. Thanks for the company.
milestone_500: 500. You really do like me. Bzzt.
Half a thousand. Half a Roman cohort!
milestone_1000: ONE THOUSAND. That's a lot of attention. Thank you.
Roman mile = 1,000 paces. We've walked one together.
A thousand! Caesar conquered Gaul with less attention than this.
100_clicks_unlock: ONE HUNDRED CLICKS. I have unlocked a chef's hat. Why? I do not know.
Achievement: chef hat. Reasons: unclear. Joy: high.
trajan_quote_corpus: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishments.
We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this.
The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
What we do in life echoes in eternity.

Hey there! Mind if I hang around?

Hi there. I tell Roman jokes. Wanna let me in?

task_completed: Roman senators wish they were as efficient.
Marcus Aurelius style: 'Do it now.' You did.
I love it when plans come together.
task_overdue_passive: Pssst. Something's been waiting a while.

streak_milestone: Augustus reigned 41 years. You're warming up.

offer_brain: Want to chat with me? I have two minds.

describe_trajan: Trajan: stoic emperor. Bias toward action.
Trajan was Spain-born, decisive, beloved of his soldiers.
Trajan mode: practical wisdom. Less philosophy, more action.

describe_providentia: Providentia weighs futures. Trajan acts on the present.
pick_trajan_warning: Heads up: Trajan is direct. Sometimes blunt.

song_accepted: Roman lyres were nice. So is this.
song_ended: Song ended. Hope you liked it!
sneeze: Romans said 'salus!' after sneezes. Bless me.

Ma'a salama! Arabic — peace go with you.

Mangkin! Wait that's not a word. Sorry.
translations: 'Star' in Latin: stella. Same as the beer.
'Bzzt' in Latin: zummum-zummum (NOT REAL, I made that up).
context_home: Home view. The atrium of NEXUS.
context_clean: Duties! Vestal Virgins kept the sacred fire spotless.
Cleaning checklist. Romans worshipped Cloacina, goddess of drains.
Sanitation matters. Pliny would lecture us.
Roman bathhouses had heated floors. We have mops.
Cleanliness is the eighth Roman virtue. Made that up.
Marcus Aurelius would file these reports.
Cleaning duty: a small empire of order.
context_log: Log view! Romans kept extensive records.
Tabulae — Roman wax tablets for logging.
Cicero would dictate this entire screen.
Pliny the Elder logged everything. 37 volumes.
Logs make history. Suetonius gossiped from records.
Every entry matters. So they said in Rome.
context_board: Board view! Like a Roman senate's whiteboard. If they had whiteboards.
Columns of tasks. Trajan's column had stories.
Bzzt — the trireme of productivity. Multi-rowed.
Sort like a quartermaster. Romans loved supplies.
Caesar planned campaigns this way. Mostly.
Each card is a small conquest.
context_cal: Roman calendar started in March. Until Caesar fixed it.
Leap year? Caesar's idea. Pope Gregory tweaked it.
Days are short, eternity is long. Roman truth.
Saturnalia is in late December. Mark it!
Romans had 100+ holidays a year. Productivity questionable.
Augustus stole a day from February for August. Stingy.
context_equipment: Equipment! Roman aqueducts ran 60+ miles by gravity.
If it's broken, fix it. Roman discipline.
Trajan's column is 100 feet of equipment-care.
Equipment care matters. Vespasian taxed urine.
Build to last. Roman concrete heals itself.
Pumps, pipes, gears. All Roman in spirit.
When it breaks, gather supplies and rebuild.
context_education: School of NEXUS! Romans had grammaticus then rhetor.

Per aspera ad astra. Through hardship to the stars.

Knowledge is the empire that doesn't fall.
Pliny the Elder read 2,000 books. You can read one.
context_train: Training! Roman legions trained 4 hours a day, every day.
Discipline is the path. Stoic truth.
Roman soldiers marched 20 miles with 60-lb packs. Hydrate.
Bzzt — train the mind, train the body.
Gladius and pilum. Practice your equivalents.

Stock like a Roman aedile (market officer).

Roman trade went to India. Order responsibly.

context_brain: NEXUS brain mode. Marcus Aurelius would meditate.

Seneca would be at home here.

Each star is something we shared.
